title: Exact rational arithmetic silently degrades to double once a bignum is involved
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). (/ 1 (expt 10 18)) is an exact rational but (/ 1 (expt 10 19)) -> 1e-19 (inexact double); (/ 1 (expt 10 100)) -> 1e-100. Worse, exact+exact loses exactness when the result denominator overflows int64: (+ (/ 1 3) (/ 1 (expt 2 62))) -> 0.333333 (double). R7RS: operations on exact operands must yield exact results or signal an error - silent exactness loss corrupts exact-fraction algorithms (SICP 2.1 rational package at scale, symbolic math). Rational runtime needs bignum numerator/denominator support (the bignum layer already exists).
acceptance: tests/stress/found/rational_bignum_exactness.esk prints OK rational-bignum-exact under -r and AOT
campaign_payoff: Completes the numeric tower: bignum integers exist but exact rationals cap at int64 components.

verifications.evidence: rational_bignum_exactness.esk still prints FAIL under -r and AOT: exact rational arithmetic degrades to inexact double once denominator exceeds int64 (bignum). Stress XKNOWN r+aot.
